schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

identity.canonical_title: Connecting BAO → Filament Inflow → Disk Mode Seeding: F_rm Ext(R,t)

theory.canonical_statement: 1. BAO/large-scale modes imprint coherent tidal fields \(T_{ij}\) and filament geometry. 2. Filaments channel anisotropic mass inflow toward halos with preferred directions (velocity streams aligned with filament). The inflow rate and direction modulate the time-dependent external forcing term on the disk: \(F_{\rm ext}(R,t)\). 3. The disk responds to this forcing via its density-wave spectrum: modes that match the forcing frequency/wavenumber are amplified (resonant excitation). 4. Result: disk “crystalization” into patterns whose spacing and orientation statistically correlate with the phase and orientation of large-scale BAO-derived modes.
theory.technical_interpretation: Connecting BAO → Filament Inflow → Disk Mode Seeding: F_rm Ext(R,t) is a symbolic expression, written F_rm ext(R,t). It introduces a symbol or relation used elsewhere in the framework. It is built from F_ ext, R. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.note: Introduced in the derivation where the inflow rate and direction modulate the time-dependent external forcing term on the disk
